ps140: What shall we call you, O glorious one?* Offering of Hellas, in that you hailed from there?* Cleansing of Phoenicia, in that you were martyred there?* Lamp, for you shone forth in the darkness?* Valiant one, in that you were not vanquished by those who beat you?* Far-reaching is your fame, O most blessed Leontius!* Pray that our souls be saved.

What shall we name you, O passion-bearer?* Soldier of Christ, in that you were a destroyer of the enemy?* Ruler of the passions, in that you were an athlete of piety?* Feeder of the hungry, in that you loved the poor?* Righteous one, in that you were a zealot of heavenly wisdom?* Diverse were your sufferings and most splendid your contest.* Pray that our souls be saved.

Who does not marvel at you, O Leontius?* For, receiving a well-spring of incorruption as a dweller in paradise,* you pour forth rivers of benefaction upon the faithful,* without fee you offer abundant gifts to all those who thirst,* and gladden those who partake of the waters of goodness.* Pray that our souls be saved.

O all-praised athlete Leontius,* neither fire, nor wounding, nor the sword were able to separate you from the love of Christ;* but, suffering manfully in the midst of the tormentors,* you abolished the offerings made to idols,* O divinely wise crown-bearer,* and now you dance in the heavens.** Earnestly pray for our souls.
stavrotheotokion: Standing before the Cross of your Son and God,* and witnessing His long-suffering, O pure Mother,* weeping, you said:* Woe is me, O my most sweet Child!* How is it that You endure these things unjustly inflicted upon You, O Word of God,* that You may save mankind?
aposticha: Let heaven be glad in splendor today, and let us earthly faithful rejoice in the memory of the martyr Leontius; for this day which hath arrived is crowned, not with irrational sacrifices, but with the sacrifice of praise, with spiritual flowers. For, standing with us, adorned, offering the un-bloody service to the Bestower of crowns, he doth radiantly offer up entreaty, that those who faithfully keep his memory may attain unto the courts of paradise.
stavrotheotokion_aposticha: The all-pure one, beholding Christ,* the Lover of mankind, crucified,* His side pierced by a spear,* cried out, weeping:* “What is this, O my Son?* How have the ungrateful people repaid You for the good things You have done for them,* that they hasten to leave me childless, O most beloved One?* I marvel at Your voluntary crucifixion,** O compassionate One!”
